eventMetadata.context_description: Emmanuel Macron's second and final presidential term concludes in May 2027 under France's constitutional limit of two consecutive terms. He publicly confirmed in April 2026 that he will exit politics entirely upon leaving the Élysée Palace. Persistent parliamentary fragmentation since the 2024 snap elections has produced minority governments and no-confidence pressures, yet these have not prompted resignation signals or early departure mechanisms. The next presidential vote is scheduled for 2027, with any potential legislative dissolution or coalition shifts unlikely to alter the fixed presidential timeline absent unforeseen health or political crises. Traders assess early exit scenarios as remote given these structural and stated constraints.

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer holds the power to request a snap general election at any time before the automatic dissolution deadline in August 2029. Recent local elections on 7 May 2026 delivered heavy losses for Labour and notable gains for Reform UK, heightening internal party pressure on Starmer and raising questions about his leadership stability. These results, combined with ongoing economic challenges and shifting voter sentiment toward smaller parties, represent the main short-term catalysts that could prompt an earlier call. Without a change in government or major crisis, however, the default timeline favors the full term, as historical precedent shows prime ministers rarely dissolve parliament early absent clear political advantage. Upcoming by-elections and polling trends will serve as key indicators for any shift in timing.

title: China x India military clash by...?
description: This is a market on the likelihood of a military clash between China and India occurring by December 31.

markets.question: China x India military clash by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of China (People's Republic of China) and India between January 29, and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between Chinese and Indian military forces. Hand-to-hand combat with the use of melee weapons between military personnel, will qualify. For example the 2020 Galwan Valley clash and the 2022 Yangtse clash would both count.

Non-violent actions, such as warning shots, artillery fire into uninhabited areas, or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution, however minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not.

Note: the China Coast Guard (CCG) is part of the military, however the Indian Coast Guard (ICG) is not.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: India and China have pursued sustained diplomatic and military engagement to stabilize their disputed Himalayan border along the Line of Actual Control. Following the 2024 patrolling pact and troop disengagements in eastern Ladakh, the two sides held their 35th Working Mechanism for Consultation and Coordination meeting in Beijing on May 27, 2026, describing the discussions as constructive and forward-looking. Officials expressed satisfaction with maintained peace and tranquillity, agreeing to use existing channels for any ground issues while advancing talks on border delimitation and trans-border river management. These steps build on resumed flights, limited trade restoration, and senior-level contacts, reflecting a tactical emphasis on de-escalation amid broader strategic competition. Scheduled follow-up mechanisms could further influence near-term risk assessments.

title: NATO/EU troops fighting in Ukraine by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if active military personnel officially affiliated with any NATO or EU country enter Ukraine for combat-related military purposes directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, they must be 1) officially acknowledged as active military by the NATO or EU entity or member state they are affiliated with; 2) be publicly acknowledged by NATO or an EU-affiliated entity to have entered Ukraine for a combat-related military purpose directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia.

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution they need be active duty and acknowledged as described above. Participation in a combat role is necessary for this market to resolve to "Yes" (e.g. military personnel providing training or intelligence support would not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, however drone pilots or infantry directly attacking Russian troops, or soldiers targeting and downing missiles from Ukrainian soil would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: European leaders have advanced proposals for multinational forces from NATO and EU member states to serve as post-ceasefire reassurance troops in Ukraine under any peace agreement, with the UK and France coordinating a coalition of the willing that includes pledges from multiple allies. Training programs for Ukrainian personnel are scheduled in three EU countries during 2026 with NATO backing, while European defense budgets and capabilities continue to expand in response to reduced US force posture on the continent. Russia has repeatedly rejected any deployment of NATO or EU troops on Ukrainian soil as unacceptable security guarantees, citing risks of escalation. No official commitments exist for direct combat participation by Western forces, and alliance statements continue to stress weapons supplies and coordination rather than ground intervention. Upcoming NATO-EU joint sessions in September and December 2026 could clarify support parameters.

eventMetadata.context_description: Keir Starmer faces intense internal pressure within the Labour Party following heavy losses in the May 2026 local and devolved elections, which prompted more than 95 MPs to demand his resignation or a clear departure timetable. Several cabinet ministers and junior aides, including Health Secretary Wes Streeting, have resigned in protest, citing policy drift and poor polling. Starmer has pledged to contest any leadership challenge and continue governing, backed by over 100 supportive MPs, while potential successors such as Andy Burnham position themselves through upcoming by-elections. Labour Party rules require a substantial number of MP nominations to trigger a formal contest, with the membership ultimately deciding. Starmer’s persistently low approval ratings and Reform UK’s rising support add further uncertainty to his tenure ahead of the 2029 general election.

title: Ukraine recognizes Russian sovereignty over its territory by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a deal in which Ukraine agrees to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory is reached between the Russian Federation and Ukraine by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The deal must be a publicly announced mutual agreement. 

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995, “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia, which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement, is an example of a qualifying agreement.

An official unilateral pledge by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution even if not part of an agreement with Russia.

The April 19, 2013, Brussels Agreement between Serbia and Kosovo, in which Serbia recognized Kosovo’s de facto administration but did not formally grant de jure recognition, would not be considered a qualifying agreement under this market, as this market requires formal recognition of sovereignty rather than acknowledgment of administrative control.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine and/or the Russian Federation, however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if a deal in which Ukraine agrees to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory is reached between the Russian Federation and Ukraine by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The deal must be a publicly announced mutual agreement. 

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995, “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia, which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement, is an example of a qualifying agreement.

An official unilateral pledge by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution even if not part of an agreement with Russia.

The April 19, 2013, Brussels Agreement between Serbia and Kosovo, in which Serbia recognized Kosovo’s de facto administration but did not formally grant de jure recognition, would not be considered a qualifying agreement under this market, as this market requires formal recognition of sovereignty rather than acknowledgment of administrative control.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine and/or the Russian Federation, however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine’s consistent refusal to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any of its 1991 territory remains the central obstacle in ongoing peace negotiations, sustaining trader consensus against such recognition occurring by December 31, 2026. Moscow continues to condition any ceasefire or deal on de jure acceptance of its claims to Crimea and the four annexed regions, while Kyiv has repeatedly affirmed its borders as non-negotiable and indicated any territorial concession would require a national referendum unlikely to pass. U.S.-mediated talks through early 2026 produced incremental progress only on prisoner exchanges, with no movement on core territorial demands amid Ukraine’s reported net battlefield gains and slowed Russian advances in April 2026. These dynamics, combined with strong domestic opposition in Ukraine to concessions lacking credible security guarantees, explain the low implied probability reflected in current pricing.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's constitution prohibits national elections, including presidential, while martial law remains in effect, which parliament has extended for the 19th time until August 2, 2026. A U.S.-pushed deadline for a vote by May 15, 2026, passed without action amid logistical barriers such as displaced voters, troops at the front, and the need for six months of preparation after hostilities end, according to the Central Election Commission. No major legislative changes to enable voting under current conditions have advanced.

title: Will any country leave NATO by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any member state formally withdraws from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

A country's exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". That country must either withdraw or submit a notice of denunciation to trigger a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source will be official information from the relevant government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tensions within NATO stem primarily from U.S. administration dissatisfaction with European allies' limited support for operations tied to the Iran conflict, including refusals to assist in reopening the Strait of Hormuz. This has prompted announced U.S. troop withdrawals from Germany and halted deployments to Poland, alongside broader contingency planning by European members to strengthen their defense pillar and reduce reliance on U.S. forces over time. No member state has issued a formal Article 13 withdrawal notice, and U.S. exit would require congressional approval under existing law. Speculation centers on potential symbolic moves by Hungary or strains involving Turkey, though neither has advanced formal steps. The alliance's July summit in Turkey represents a key upcoming diplomatic forum that could shape alliance cohesion or further adjustments in commitments.

title: What will happen before GTA VI?
description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

markets.question: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if  if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution for a ceasefire will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if it Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source will be official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ official November 19, 2026 release date for Grand Theft Auto VI, reaffirmed in recent Take-Two earnings guidance, has locked in trader consensus that the open-world title will miss any June 2026 window. Multiple prior delays—from an initial Fall 2025 target to May 2026 and then to November—reflect the studio’s emphasis on additional polish, with no credible signals of an accelerated launch or surprise early drop. At 99.9% implied probability for “No,” the market reflects overwhelming evidence from verified announcements and fiscal projections. The only realistic upset would require an unprecedented last-minute shift that contradicts current development timelines and corporate statements.

title: Harvey Weinstein prison time?
description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrial on the Jessica Mann rape charge, declared May 15 after a deadlocked jury, has solidified trader sentiment around “No Prison Time” at 76% implied probability. Prosecutors must now decide by late June whether to pursue a fourth attempt, introducing further delays and uncertainty for additional convictions. Weinstein, already serving a 16-year California sentence from his 2022 conviction while appealing that verdict, faces separate pending New York sentencing on another count but benefits from his advanced age, documented health issues, and the high bar for retrials in closely watched cases. Historical patterns of overturned or limited outcomes in his proceedings, combined with ongoing appeals, reinforce the market’s view that significant new prison time remains unlikely in the near term.

title: Will OpenAI launch a consumer hardware product by...?
description: This is a market on whether OpenAI will release a consumer hardware product by the specified date.

markets.question: Will OpenAI launch a new consumer hardware product in 2025?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI publicly announces and launches a new consumer hardware product by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

A "consumer hardware product" is defined as a physical device intended for direct use by individual consumers, rather than enterprise or developer tools. The product must be newly introduced and not a rebrand, update, or iteration of any previously released device.

Examples that would qualify include an AI-powered wearable, smart home assistant, or augmented reality device. Developer tools, AI chips, or servers marketed exclusively for enterprise use would not qualify.

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpenAI’s January 2026 confirmation that it remains on track for a second-half reveal of its first consumer AI device, following the 2025 acquisition of Jony Ive’s io startup, serves as the main catalyst shaping trader views. The company has over 200 people developing a family of products, with early prototypes described as screenless, voice-first wearables or a smart speaker featuring cameras for contextual awareness and local inference via custom chips. While a late-2026 announcement appears likely, credible reporting indicates actual shipments could slip into early 2027, amid parallel rumors of an AI smartphone targeted for 2027–2028 mass production. Competitive moves by Apple and supply-chain signals around processors add further uncertainty to near-term launch thresholds.

title: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by...?
description: This is a market on the likelihood of Russia capturing Kostyantynivka by a specific date.

markets.question: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by August 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Kostyantynivka railroad station located on Pravoberezhna vulytsia by August 31, 2025 at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by August 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/hZ6ch5jVRvkNFY317

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have intensified offensive operations around Kostyantynivka in Donetsk Oblast since early 2026, employing infiltration tactics, glide bombs, and small-unit assaults to probe Ukrainian positions on the city's outskirts as part of broader efforts to outflank the Kramatorsk-Slovyansk defensive line. Geolocated reports through late May confirm incremental Russian advances south, east, and southeast, including near Illinivka, though Ukrainian forces have repelled most attempts to establish a firm foothold and conducted local counter-moves while relying on drones and artillery to disrupt Russian logistics. The city, already heavily damaged with most civilians evacuated, forms a key node in Ukraine's eastern fortifications, and trader sentiment reflects measured confidence in eventual capture by year-end amid slow but persistent Russian pressure and limited major breakthroughs in recent weeks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez has consistently signaled his intent to complete the current legislative term through the scheduled general election in 2027, citing economic stability and foreign policy priorities despite coalition fragility with parties including Junts. Recent regional votes, including conservative gains and PSOE setbacks in Andalusia in May 2026 and earlier contests in Aragon and Extremadura, have highlighted a rightward shift without prompting dissolution of the Cortes Generales. Ongoing corruption investigations and opposition pressure for early polls have not altered Sánchez’s public rejection of a snap election, while uncertainty over the 2026 General State Budget and Junts support remains the main potential trigger. Trader consensus reflects these factors, with no dissolution announced by late May 2026.

title: US x Russia military clash by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military encounter between the military forces of the United States and Russia between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military encounter" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between U.S. and Russian military forces. Non-violent actions, such as airspace violations, firing of warning shots (such as the June, 2021 Black Sea Confrontations between Russian forces and HMS Defender), or cyberattacks will not qualify. 

Intentional physical collisions, including aerial interceptions and naval ramming without the direct use of weaponry, such as the 2023 Black Sea incident—where a Russian Su-27 damaged a U.S. MQ-9 Reaper drone's propeller, leading to its crash— will not qualify regardless of damage. 

Military contractors will qualify only if confirmed to be operating under the direct command or coordination of the respective state’s armed forces (e.g. the Battle of Khasham would not qualify).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing proxy support for Ukraine amid the Russia-Ukraine conflict, combined with recent Russian drone incursions into Romanian territory and intensified strikes on Kyiv, has sustained NATO alerts without triggering direct US-Russia engagement. Reestablished high-level US-Russia military-to-military talks since February 2026 aim to mitigate miscalculation risks, while senior Russian diplomats have flagged rising escalation dangers with NATO. US mediation efforts and alliance commitments to defend member states have kept bilateral military clashes off the immediate horizon. These factors underpin trader consensus on low near-term probabilities for direct confrontation by mid- or end-2026, though incidents near borders or shifts in Western aid could alter dynamics.

title: Will Russia invade a NATO country by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Russia commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of any NATO country between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by NATO member states or Russia as of May 28, 2025, will be considered the sovereign territory of those countries.

Hybrid or “grey zone” operations conducted by unmarked personnel will qualify if they are confirmed to be Russian operatives and are intended to establish de facto control over territory, as occurred in Crimea (2014) or Donbas (2014–2022).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russia continues its full-scale war in Ukraine while conducting hybrid operations against NATO members, including drone incursions into Latvian airspace and sabotage incidents reported in multiple eastern flank states. Dutch military intelligence assessed in April 2026 that Moscow could rebuild sufficient forces for a limited regional challenge to NATO within one year after fighting in Ukraine ends, with goals centered on territorial gains and alliance division rather than outright defeat. NATO military leaders have highlighted risks of a test of Article 5 by 2028–2029, citing Russia’s ongoing buildup and gray-zone tactics such as unmarked forces or proxies. Polish officials recently described the threat window in months rather than years, emphasizing the need for rapid political and logistical readiness on the eastern flank. These developments, alongside stalled ceasefire efforts and continued Russian strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure, shape trader focus on timelines, escalation thresholds, and alliance cohesion.

title: Trump eliminates capital gains tax on crypto by ___?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump signs a bill into law or performs any executive action changing US tax law to eliminate capital gains tax on any cryptocurrency assets by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Such a bill must apply to US taxpaying persons in general; if a law applies only to specific companies or institutions, it will not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market.

If capital gains is eliminated only for specific types or classes of crypto assets (e.g., only Bitcoin or Ethereum, only American issued cryptocurrencies, or only NFTs), it will still count toward a "Yes" resolution for this market.

If the bill is signed into law, or executive action is instituted before the resolution date, it will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of when it takes effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US federal government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-mediated security talks between Israel and Syria intensified after the December 2024 fall of the Assad regime, leading to a January 2026 joint communication mechanism for intelligence sharing, military coordination, and dispute resolution under the 1974 disengagement framework. These channels address border de-escalation and southern Syria stability but have not produced formal diplomatic recognition. Syrian officials, including President Ahmed al-Sharaa, prioritize sovereignty and territorial claims including the Golan Heights, while Israel emphasizes security guarantees and demilitarization. Ongoing US involvement and periodic high-level contacts continue, yet persistent mistrust and unresolved core issues limit prospects for full normalization by late 2026.

markets.question: Will Russia capture Sumy by March 31, 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Sumy railroad station located on Pryvokzalna ploscha by March 31, 2027, at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue limited offensive operations in northern Sumy Oblast to establish buffer zones along the border, with recent geolocated evidence showing small advances and infiltration near villages such as Popivka, Vysoke, and areas around Ryasne and Zapsillya. Ukrainian forces have refuted Russian claims of capturing several settlements and maintain control over most key positions closer to Sumy city, while conducting drone strikes that hinder further Russian movement. No tactically significant advances toward the city itself have occurred in recent weeks, reflecting Ukrainian defensive efforts and Russian prioritization of incremental gains over a direct assault on the urban center. Ongoing artillery, drone, and missile activity persists amid conflicting battlefield reports.

The Vegas Golden Knights, at 41.4%, advanced by sweeping the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche and have posted a 19-5 mark since John Tortorella assumed head coaching duties in late March. Both clubs enter the Final as the hottest teams remaining, with recent series momentum and coaching stability outweighing earlier regular-season positioning in current market pricing.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France lead the 2026 World Cup winner market in a tight cluster with England, Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina close behind due to their deep squads, recent form in UEFA Nations League and qualifiers, and star power including Kylian Mbappe and Lamine Yamal. Spain edged ahead after positive updates on Yamal's hamstring recovery from his club season, restoring their slight edge over France following the December group draw. Multiple European sides benefit from strong FIFA rankings, home-continent familiarity in the expanded 48-team format, and proven tournament experience, while South American contenders like Argentina and Brazil maintain realistic paths despite longer travel. This balance of elite depth and minimal separation in current form sustains the competitive implied probabilities across the top group.
